patches.summary: Use resultsPerPage for YesWeHack page-size parameters instead of itemsPerPage on researcher-facing list endpoints.
patches.reason: The web API ignores itemsPerPage for programs, user reports, events, ranking, and user members; resultsPerPage is the parameter used by the browser and returns the requested page size.
patches.validated_outcome: programs?resultsPerPage=100 returned all 69 currently visible programs on one page, while itemsPerPage was ignored and returned the default 42 rows.

patches.summary: Teach live --all reads and sync to follow page/nb_pages pagination envelopes.
patches.reason: The generated pagination helper only followed cursor-style pagination, so --all stopped after the first YesWeHack page even when pagination.nb_pages reported more pages.
patches.validated_outcome: Unit tests cover page-style pagination, and sync can now advance using pagination.page and pagination.nb_pages.

patches.summary: Expose the browser invitation-page endpoints for hunter membership and current access programs.
patches.reason: user list-invitations reads pending invitations only. The YesWeHack invitation page also uses /user/members for membership records and /v2/hunter/access/programs for programs the hunter can currently access.
patches.validated_outcome: /user/members returned 6 membership records and /v2/hunter/access/programs returned the current access-program set for the authenticated hunter.

patches.summary: Prefix hunter-access-program IDs before local caching.
patches.reason: Access-program rows use program slugs as identifiers, which collide with the programs resource in the generic resources table where id is globally unique.
patches.validated_outcome: hunter-access-programs rows are stored under resource-scoped IDs such as hunter-access-programs|<slug> instead of overwriting programs rows.

patches.summary: Cache per-program scope rows as program-scopes with stable local IDs and program slug annotations.
patches.reason: programs list-scopes returned rows without generic id/slug/name fields, so write-through caching attempted to store them as programs and skipped every scope row.
patches.validated_outcome: programs list-scopes can now write scope rows into the local store under program-scopes with stable IDs.
